title: digitaria mutica
intro: Digitaria mutica, also known as Dallis grass or pangola grass, is a type of invasive perennial grass native to tropical Africa. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Digitaria mutica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction by boiling the leaves and stems in water, or by making a paste from the plant's roots and applying it topically to affected areas.
